questions.question: chmod doesn’t change the permissions of symbolic links, since the chmod system call cannot change their permissions on most systems, and most systems ignore permissions of symbolic links.
questions.explanation: chmod doesn’t change the permissions of symbolic links, since the chmod system call cannot change their permissions on most systems, and most systems ignore permissions of symbolic links.

questions.question: chmod doesn’t change symbolic link listed on the command line, chmod changes the permissions of the pointed-to file.
questions.explanation: chmod doesn’t change the permissions of symbolic links, since the chmod system call cannot change their permissions on most systems, and most systems ignore permissions of symbolic links.

questions.question: However, for each symbolic link listed on the command line, chmod changes the permissions of the pointed-to file.
questions.explanation: However, for each symbolic link listed on the command line, chmod changes the permissions of the pointed-to file.

questions.question: However, for each ignores symbolic links encountered during recursive directory traversals.

questions.question: In contrast, chmod ignores symbolic links encountered during recursive directory traversals.

questions.question: Options that modify whose effective user ID matches the user ID of the file, or a process with appropriate privileges, is permitted to change the file mode bits of a file.

questions.question: Only a process whose effective user ID matches the user ID of the file, or a process with appropriate privileges, is permitted to change the file mode bits of a file.
questions.explanation: Only a process whose effective user ID matches the user ID of the file, or a process with appropriate privileges, is permitted to change the file mode bits of a file.

questions.question: Only a process of chmod clears the set-group-ID bit of a regular file if the file’s group ID does not match the user’s effective group ID or one of the user’s supplementary group IDs, unless the user has appropriate privileges.
questions.explanation: Only a process whose effective user ID matches the user ID of the file, or a process with appropriate privileges, is permitted to change the file mode bits of a file.
